title: Track every vector's coordinate space
description: Be explicit about object, world, view, and clip space and apply the model-view-projection chain in the right order so geometry lands where intended.
body: Use when positioning, lighting, or projecting geometry. Do not multiply a vector by a matrix from the wrong space, and avoid mixing row-major and column-major conventions between the math library and the shader. Actions: label each position/normal with its space, apply model (object->world), view (world->camera), then projection (camera->clip) in that order, and transform normals by the inverse-transpose of the model matrix. Validation signals include geometry appearing at the expected screen location and a reference-image match within tolerance. Risk boundary: a transposed or mis-ordered matrix can still render a plausible image while being geometrically wrong; verify against a known reference rather than eyeballing.
